title: Cold War vs War On Terror
shortAnswer: The Cold War was a decades-long ideological standoff with the Soviet Union, while the War on Terror is a global military campaign against terrorist networks initiated after 9/11.

keyDifferences.label: Geographic Scope
keyDifferences.entityBValue: Multiple regions including Middle East, Caribbean, Eastern Pacific

prosA: Maintained nuclear deterrence without direct superpower war
Established clear ideological and geographic boundaries
consA: Led to numerous costly proxy wars
Created decades of global tension and fear
Massive military spending and arms race
prosB: Direct response to attacks on American soil
consB: Ongoing with no clear end in sight
Expanded to multiple theaters without clear victory conditions
High financial and human costs over two decades
verdict: The Cold War was a more defined conflict with clear adversaries and eventual resolution, while the War on Terror represents a more complex, ongoing global campaign against dispersed non-state actors. Both conflicts fundamentally reshaped American foreign policy and global security architecture.
